decisions.title: Owner ratifies the 14-clause source-to-runtime ownership contract
decisions.decision: All 14 non-negotiable target-state clauses in yazelix_runtime_convergence/PLAN.md are ratified as-is. This clears the YZXCONV-002 human-approval gate and unblocks Waves 1-3. Ratification is authority to design and build to the contract; each mutating step (profile closure, agent config, branch publication) still requires its own per-step owner approval and rollback receipt. No blanket apply authority is granted.

decisions.title: Strict-Nushell agent routing via nu-MCP + deny-Bash + nu-syntax skill (two swaps + hard gate)
decisions.refinement_note: Refined after the yazelix + nushell-MCP deep-research pass (owner-requested). The original nu -c PreToolUse rewrite mechanism is REPLACED by nu-MCP + deny-Bash + nu-syntax skill; the rewrite hook is demoted to a rejected/documented-broken option. yazelix itself was confirmed NOT to route the agent's internal shell tool through nu (it launches the agent in a nu pane only); the routing technique comes from the MCP side of the ecosystem, not from yazelix.
decisions.decision: Clause 11 (agents execute shell only through Nushell) is achieved by the research-proven TWO-SWAPS-PLUS-HARD-GATE model, NOT by a nu -c rewrite hook: (1) SYNTAX SWAP - a vendored nushell-syntax skill so each agent emits Nushell rather than bash; (2) INTERPRETER ROUTING - a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer the built-in nu --mcp from Nushell 0.108+, else a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate that respects the no-C boundary) into which the model writes nu as a typed argument; (3) HARD GATE - deny/disable the built-in Bash tool so no bash escape remains. The nu -c PreToolUse rewrite hook is REJECTED as the primary mechanism: it is broken by claude-code #4535 (dev-null injection), unreliable per #15897/#26506 (updatedInput), and fundamentally hands bash syntax to a non-POSIX nu parser (verified locally on nu 0.113.1: andand, dollar-paren, 2>&1, and heredocs all fail). IRREDUCIBLE FLOOR: this is a feature-swap not a transparent shim - the agent must speak nu; bash-emitting commands fail loudly rather than being translated (no bash-to-nu transpiler exists); syntax enforcement is probabilistic and coverage is bounded (tools that hardcode bash break or slip). Wrapper-lane convergence (interactive nu panes + rtk command rewriting) remains the documented partial fallback. Implemented by YZXCONV-028 under owner approval.
evidence: Local verification on nu 0.113.1: bash '&&', '$(...)', '2>&1', and heredocs all fail at the nushell parser when passed to nu -c; nushell is explicitly non-POSIX (nushell.sh cookbook/foreign_shell_scripts)
anthropics/claude-code #4535: the Bash tool appends '< /dev/null', which breaks any nu -c wrapping
anthropics/claude-code #15897 and #26506: PreToolUse updatedInput is unreliable / unsupported across paths
openai/codex #1708 and #4210: exec force-wraps [shell_path, -lc, script] with a bash body; #3823 has no supported shell-selector key

Nushell ships a built-in MCP server (nu --mcp) - CONFIRMED present in the profile nu 0.113.1 on 2026-07-14 (version|get features includes 'mcp'; nu --help shows --mcp, --mcp-transport stdio/http, --mcp-port), so the execution half is already profile-owned with no build or vendored crate needed; external cablehead/mcp-server-nu and ck3mp3r/nu-mcp remain fallbacks

luccahuguet/yazelix launches the managed-agent popup via TWO real, contract-checked config keys - agent.command (executable-or-auto, default auto) and agent.args (string array, default []) - verified in yazelix source (crates/yzx-config/src/catalog.rs:8-9, root_config.rs agent_command/agent_args/render_agent_popup_kdl, runtime/yzx/runtime.rs:34-35,97-98, ARCHITECTURE.md:113-114, CHANGELOG.md:111-112). This is a declarative LAUNCH lever (command + args) and sets shell.program=nu for new panes; it launches the agent in a nu pane but does NOT reach the agent's INTERNAL Bash tool, so it does not by itself route the agent's tool through nu. Correction: an earlier web-summary agent surfaced only agent.command and missed agent.args.
nushell-prophet/nushell-skills (Claude Code plugin marketplace) and hustcer/nushell-pro (SKILL.md) provide the agent nu-syntax skill - the syntax-swap half

decisions.title: The Nushell command catalog wraps external binaries; it does not reimplement them
decisions.decision: The native Nushell command catalog (YZXCONV-010/011/012) is a set of Nu externs / thin --wrapped defs + carapace completions that CALL external binaries through the single rtk owner - NOT reimplementations in Nushell or Rust. Canonical shape (rtk_wrappers.nu): `export def --wrapped gh [...rest] { ^rtk gh ...$rest }`; completions come from carapace (`carapace _carapace nushell`), not per-command hand-written externs. Scoping consequence: for every external CLI (gh, meta, grit, icm, weave, nix, docker, bun, ...) the refactor adds a nu extern/wrapper/completion and routes through rtk; it MUST NOT rewrite the tool's logic. gh specifically is a statically-linked Go binary (gh 2.96.0), not bash - the only 'bash' is its unused upstream completion scripts (replaced by carapace) and the bash -lc launcher that YZXCONV-011/017 retire.
evidence: yazelix/nushell/config/rtk_wrappers.nu:18 (gh wrapper) + header lines 1-13 ('one executable owner ... without a second wrapper binary or plugin bridge')
rtk-tokenkill/src/cmds/git/gh_cmd.rs resolved_command('gh')/run_passthrough('gh'); cmds/README.md:11 ('executes an external command')
/usr/bin/gh = Go ELF, cli/cli v2.96.0; yazelix/defaults/nu/config.nu:1 sources @carapaceInit@ generated at flake.nix:178
task_graph.source.csv YZXCONV-010/012 already specify 'native Nu modules and externs' resolving 'through profile paths'

decisions.title: Yazi Lua plugins are an accepted third-party plugin surface, not language drift
decisions.decision: Yazi's plugin API is mandatorily Lua (a Yazi plugin cannot be authored in Rust), so the vendored Yazi Lua plugins and flavor assets are an ACCEPTED third-party plugin surface - the same class as the libSQL lemon.c build-time exception (the upheld tenet is a linkage law - no C library linked into the trust boundary - not a source-language law). yazelix-yazi-assets is itself a Rust package (Cargo.toml, src/lib.rs, serde+toml) that renders/ships the Lua/TOML as payload; the workspace SOURCE stays Rust. Yazi Lua is outside the shell-convergence law (which targets Nushell shell contract + retiring Bash/Zsh routes), so the pure-Rust / no-non-Rust-source drift check MUST NOT flag Yazi Lua plugins.
evidence: yazelix-yazi-assets/plugins/{git,lazygit,starship,auto-layout,smart-tabs}.yazi/main.lua + 23 flavors; yazelix/defaults/yazi/{init.lua,plugins/*.yazi/main.lua}
yazelix-yazi-assets Cargo.toml (name yazelix_yazi_assets, edition 2024, [lib] src/lib.rs, serde+toml) - a Rust renderer shipping Lua assets
envctl/crates/secrets-store-libsql/README.md:87,103 lemon.c precedent ('accept it'; 'nothing C is linked')
AGENTS.md: 'Preserve upstream plugin license files when refreshing vendored plugins'

decisions.title: Home Manager is the declarative single-owner mechanism; musl proves eligible-binary portability
decisions.decision: The clean single-owner path for the whole config surface is the yazelix Home Manager module (yazelix/home-manager/module.nix): set `programs.yazelix.config.settings.shell.program = "nu"` declaratively (nu is already the shipped default - only Nushell is supported; the flake's own regression sets shell.program=nu) instead of hand-editing ~/.config/yazelix/config.toml. The module writes NO runtime config unless a key is declared (lib.mkIf cfg.enable + lib.optionalAttrs (settings != null) + filterAttrs (!= null)), and installs via a single `home.packages = [cfg.package]` (singular `package`; there is no plural programs.yazelix.packages option). Use `programs.yazelix.package = inputs.yazelix.packages.${system}.runtime` (the withMars=false headless CLI+config closure, no GUI terminal / no .desktop) to honor input/output separation (clause 2). HM must be consumed INSIDE the one lifeos_foundation_yzx element, never a second profile / edit surface / launcher shadow (clause 14, YZXCONV-022). musl (x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, toolchain already wired at flake.nix:768-800) proves portability for ELIGIBLE pure-Rust CLIs (self-contained, no glibc, no /nix/store - run on the dual-5090 Ubuntu target without Nix); GUI/Nix/native/glibc-only binaries get an explicit closure/bundle fallback, never a false universal-static claim (clause 13, YZXCONV-021).
evidence: yazelix/home-manager/module.nix (options 103-147; config guards 152-168); flake.nix homeManagerModules.default:157, runtime output yzxRuntime:662, runtime-closure checks 1204-1207, HM regression shell.program=nu:1030
yazelix/docs/configuration.md:35 ('only Nushell is supported'); defaults/config.toml shell.program=nu default
PLAN.md clauses 1/2/13/14; YZXCONV-021 (musl) and YZXCONV-022 (Home Manager) gates
musl.libc.org/about (static, no external deps); rust x86_64-unknown-linux-musl fully-static target; flake.nix:768-800 musl toolchain wired

decisions.title: agent-env owns only the repo-scoped skills+MCP baseline; the yazelix<->meta connection is the ADR-0006 home symlink farm + nix profile
decisions.decision: Source-traced correction of the 'agent-env is the central control and connection between yazelix and meta' framing. agent-env (envctl, scope: project) writes PLAIN files only into the envctl repo root - .claude/skills, .codex/skills, .mcp.json, .codex/config.toml (agent.rs:240/242/262-281) - dry-run by default, --apply to write, lock-checked (ci/gates/agent-env.sh). It does NOT write into yazelix or the nix profile and does NOT own the nushell runtime config or the live home/.codex|.claude runtime; those are hand-authored home/** sources ADR-0006-symlinked to the live home. agent-env's ONLY relationship to yazelix is a conformance constraint: the MCP baseline must be URL-only (no non-profile local-binary MCPs, 'mirror Yazelix ownership'), enforced by test-agent-mcp-yazelix-mirror.sh. So agent-env centrally controls the agent skills+MCP baseline SURFACE; the actual yazelix<->meta CONNECTION is the ADR-0006 home/** symlink farm (e.g. ~/.config/nushell/config.nu -> home/.config/nushell/config.nu whose line 6 sources the profile-owned rtk_wrappers.nu) plus the yazelix-owned nix profile. meta_plugin_protocol is IN SYNC (envctl path-dep ../../../../meta_plugin_protocol resolves to the meta root; meta-env.rs run_plugin->ExecutionPlan runs the envctl binary; meta_cli consumes it via subprocess_plugins). meta_mcp is a standalone meta_cli-backed MCP server (~30 tools) that builds but is UNWIRED (in no active agent config, absent from agent-env.yaml). meta_git_cli/lib drive real git/gh as subprocesses (no nu-def collision; only the loop_lib $SHELL -c Plan path is a nushell-only caveat). meta_core is the .meta.yaml model + ~/.meta state layer. No live skill/mcp duplication exists (old claude/codex-plugins archived-only; meta-plugins absorbed codex payloads; envctl's 5 skills are name-disjoint). Actionable drift is tracked in YZXCONV-029.
evidence: envctl crates/agent-env/src/agent.rs:240/242/262-281 (project-scope projection targets); fsops.rs:377-383 scope_root; ci/gates/agent-env.sh + scripts/tests/test-agent-mcp-yazelix-mirror.sh

ADR-0006 home symlink farm: ~/.config/nushell/config.nu -> meta/src/envctl/home/.config/nushell/config.nu (line 6 sources ~/.nix-profile/nushell/config/rtk_wrappers.nu); home/.claude/rules/toolchain.md

meta_plugin_protocol: envctl crates/cli/Cargo.toml:34 path-dep resolves to /home/flexnetos/meta/meta_plugin_protocol; crates/cli/src/bin/meta-env.rs run_plugin/ExecutionPlan; meta_cli/src/subprocess_plugins.rs + registry.rs:293 resolve_plugin_source
meta_mcp: /home/flexnetos/meta/meta_mcp bin meta-mcp (~30 tools), depends on meta_cli, unwired in any active agent config

Verified: no ~/.mcp.json; envctl/.mcp.json = cognitum/cognitum-seed/exa (url-only); active ~/.codex/config.toml = only openaiDeveloperDocs (missing exa, unaccounted); .meta.yaml stale claude-plugins/codex-plugins peers + meta-plugins path drift

decisions.title: Agent-runtime supply-chain plan: separate three-lane catalog, NO-OPTIONAL law, RuVector=npm-latest, databases MUST be in the yazelix profile
decisions.decision: Owner rulings that land the agent-runtime supply-chain plan (brainstorm ratified 2026-07-14; full design in yazelix_runtime_convergence/SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md). (1) DATA MODEL: the supply chain is a SEPARATE registry supply_chain.catalog.csv beside task_graph.source.csv with a ONE-WAY task_id foreign key INTO the task graph; the task graph is never given supply-chain columns and is never edited by catalog churn; the reverse task-to-package index is built at generation time (mirrors the existing *.source.csv -> generated/*.json pipeline). (2) NO-OPTIONAL LAW (anti-black-hole; owner CRITICAL warning): the word optional is DELETED - there is no optional, install-later, nice-to-have, or non-blocking state. Every catalog row is BUILT before DONE; optional MEANS install. An agent that treats any row as deferrable/non-blocking has entered a failure loop; optional is banned as a classification value and the catalog validator fails if the token appears. (3) RUVECTOR = NPM LATEST: latest RuVector lives in npm (CI cross-compiles and pushes to npm the moment a branch merges - blueprint lines 89-91), so npm is newest and crates.io LAGS. The latest npm build carries 200+ built-in AI agent tools; an older resource yields only ~80-120; anything but latest = fighting continuous refactoring. crates.io is the blueprint's PURITY fallback that itself admits it misses bleeding-edge features (line 91) and is DEPRIORITIZED; local meta-ruvector is the blueprint's messy scratchpad / napi-rs exhaust (lines 93-99, completely avoid cloning) and is REFERENCE ONLY (~60% stale). (4) ENUMERATION IS OPEN: the COGNITUM guides and meta-ruvector plus worktrees are an OPEN reference to explore if/when needed - do NOT freeze an exhaustive locked package list now. (5) NORTH STAR + KEYS: built to the Architecture Blueprint (hash above); registry keys (npm, crates.io) come from the secretd broker at build time, NEVER baked into the nix store (catalog records only registry_key_scope, never a value). (6) THREE LANES, ALL PROFILE-OWNED (owner correction applied): the seam is runtime shape, not in-profile-versus-out, because the profile owns the ENTIRE stack (blueprint Phase 4: compile the entire stack to musl-static, drop the nix store). LANE path = agent CLIs/tools on PATH via the yazelix runtime closure. LANE service = PostgreSQL PLUS the RuVector extension - these MUST be flake-declared IN the yazelix nix-profile and run as a profile-owned daemon (extension via shared_preload_libraries, data dir outside the store); the owner corrected the current state (no database is in the profile today) to the TARGET (the databases MUST be in the yazelix nix-profile), so DB-in-profile is a tracked GAP (YZXCONV-035), not an out-of-profile lane. LANE app-dep = libraries the app links (ruvector via bun add for the TS/Bun front-end, ruvector-core/ruvllm via cargo for the Rust daemon) - project-local pinned lockfiles built with a profile-owned toolchain; project-local is NOT a global/system install and is compliant. ruvector legitimately spans the service and app-dep lanes as different artifact forms of one project (one row per package x lane). The no-global / no-system-depth law reduces to: everything is either yazelix-profile-owned (path, service, and every toolchain) or project-local-pinned (app-dep); nothing is apt, system-depth, or npm -g. Lands as YZXCONV-034 (build the catalog) and YZXCONV-035 (postgres plus ruvector extension into the profile).
evidence: Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md lines 87-91 ('Why use NPM releases first' - CI pushes to npm on merge; crates.io lags) and 93-113 ('napi-rs exhaust', 'Fall back on Crates.io', 'completely avoid cloning the main RuVector (meta-ruvector) GitHub repository', 'might miss out on bleeding-edge features')

Architecture Blueprint lines 23-42 (Phase 4 Build: 'Use Nix to deterministically compile the entire stack into fully static musl binaries, completely dropping the Nix store dependency') and 68-79 (RuVector operates natively as a PostgreSQL extension, eliminating a sidecar vector DB)

Owner ruling 2026-07-14: 200+ AI agent tools in the latest ruvector PostgreSQL extension build vs ~80-120 for older resources; 'IT IS CRITICAL TO USE THE LATEST CODE'; local ruvector builds but is ~60% likely not-latest
Owner correction 2026-07-14: 'none of the databases are in yazelix nix-profile [today]. The Databases MUST be IN the YAZELIX NIX-PROFILE to pull this off'
README.md artifact catalog: blueprint SHA-256 014bbebb8afceee7f8deea236ed3b9425b61be3840fba47aee7c131f77268827, 70753 bytes, 707 lines

Prior chain: DEC-YZXCONV-005 (musl portability + Home Manager single-owner) and DEC-YZXCONV-006 (agent-env repo-scope surface vs ADR-0006 home symlink farm) - the supply-chain lanes ride the same single-profile / no-system-install ethos

decisions.title: Full database/store inventory adopted; registry-leader is per-artifact (verified), not blanket npm; ruvllm lane open; tasks 036-038
decisions.decided_by: Agent (deep-research loop, owner-directed) - part B flagged for owner confirmation
decisions.decision: A deep-research loop (three parallel agents: codebase store scan, blueprint cross-reference, live registry verification) landed the complete store inventory into SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8 and refines DEC-YZXCONV-007. (A) POSTGRESQL IS NOT THE ONLY DATABASE: the profile-owned stack is a tiered hierarchy - redb (microsecond buffer/scratchpad; DUAL-MODE: pass-through in the happy path and a durable RETAINING WAL when Postgres is down; plus BLAKE3 dedup + geometry engine) FLUSHES to PostgreSQL + the RuVector pgrx extension (the consolidation target / permanent source of truth), with AgentDB/.rvf as an orthogonal active reinforcement-learning memory tier; libsql-remote (secrets Store) and sqlx/SQLite (LifeOS local) also present in code. Every runtime participant in the redb->flush->Postgres pipeline is now a REQUIRED catalog row (redb, ruvector-core, ruvector-sona, ruvllm, rvf-runtime, rvf-types, hnsw_rs, sqlx-sqlite, candle-or-ort, all-MiniLM-L6-v2, nu_plugin_ruvector) so the consolidation target has its declared upstream. The owner's model - redb passthrough + dual mode, and PostgreSQL as the single source of truth - is CONFIRMED against the blueprint (lines 44-48, 245-252, 411, 424). (B) EMPIRICAL REGISTRY CORRECTION to DEC-YZXCONV-007 #3 (FLAGGED for owner confirmation): the 2026-07-14 live registry snapshot shows crates.io LEADS the RuVector ENGINE (ruvector-core 2.3.0, ruvllm 2.3.0, ruvector-postgres 2.0.5 pgrx) while npm LAGS / is bindings-only (bare ruvector 0.2.34; scoped @ruvector/* 2.x via napi-rs); @ruvnet/ruvector is a DEAD 404 handle (the real coordinates are unscoped ruvector + @ruvector/*). The '200+ AI agent tools' are ruvector-postgres's 230+ SQL functions delivered crates.io/pgrx - exactly what YZXCONV-035 packages. LAW (intent-preserving; the owner's 'use the latest code, get the 200+ tools, avoid continuous refactoring' intent is UPHELD as law): latest code wins and the catalog records the VERIFIED LEADING REGISTRY PER ARTIFACT resolved at pin time, NOT a blanket npm-always-wins. DEC-YZXCONV-007's npm-latest ruling scopes to the TS/Bun FRONT-END BINDING (ruvector / @ruvector/*) only and must NOT be over-applied to independent upstream crates (redb, candle, sqlx, libsql), which take normal crates.io pins. (C) C-BOUNDARY: rusqlite 0.32.1 bundled links C SQLite and exists ONLY in the nested home/agent-env/codex-harness sub-workspace (its own workspace, outside envctl's no-C main gate) - it is NEVER promoted into the profile; the sanctioned SQL paths are redb (pure-Rust) or libsql remote. (D) NEW TASKS (all classification=required, NO-OPTIONAL law): YZXCONV-036 seeds the full store/edge-inference/ingestion inventory into supply_chain.catalog.csv; YZXCONV-037 rules the ruvllm lane (blueprint daemon shape leans service/path) and, if service/path, packages the edge-inference runtime into the profile; YZXCONV-038 provisions the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model asset + Candle/ORT framework as a reproducible runtime artifact.
evidence: Codebase scan: envctl Cargo.toml (redb 4.1, libsql 0.9.30 remote-only, ruvector-core 2.2.3 pin, ruvllm 2.3.0, rvf-runtime 0.3.0, rvf-types 0.2.1, ruvector-postgres 2.0.5 feature-gated); meta-ruvector crates/ruvector-core/src/storage.rs redb 2.1 + crates/ruvector-postgres pgrx 0.12; lifeos crates/lifeos-core sqlx 0.8 sqlite+tls-rustls; home/agent-env/codex-harness Cargo.toml rusqlite 0.32.1 bundled (nested workspace); yazelix packaging beads_rust 0.2.16 / weave (sqlite|libsql dual) / kache

Blueprint Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md: 8 + 44-48 (redb buffer -> flush -> Postgres; application-level WAL), 245-252 (tiered hierarchy redb -> Postgres+RuVector), 268 (only significant states persisted), 270-282 (.rvf active RL memory + ruvllm runtime), 361-439 (redb technical report; 411 WAL), 424 (No-Sidecar / RuVector-as-extension), 143-149 (nu_plugin MessagePack -> redb), 161 + 575 (local embedding all-MiniLM via Candle or ORT)

Live registry verification 2026-07-14: crates.io ruvector-core 2.3.0 / ruvllm 2.3.0 / ruvector-postgres 2.0.5 (pgrx, pg14-17); npm ruvector 0.2.34, @ruvector/graph-node 2.0.4, @ruvector/attention 2.2.2 (publisher ruvnet); @ruvnet/ruvector 404; redb 4.1.0; rusqlite 0.40.1 (links C SQLite); libsql 0.9.30; postgresql 18.4; napi 3.10.5 + @napi-rs/cli 3.7.3 (per-platform gnu/musl optionalDependency); bun 1.3.14; @anthropic-ai/claude-code 2.1.209; @openai/codex 0.144.4; grit/icm/weave meta-internal (public @getgrit/cli is GritQL, a different tool)

Owner directive 2026-07-14: 'PostgreSQL is not the only database ... we have redb, sqlite, and many more'; 'redb is passthrough but can retain the current features as dual mode'; 'All data is stored in PostgreSQL ... the real refactoring/consolidation/merges will be done' - all CONFIRMED against code + blueprint

decisions.title: Owner ratifies the per-artifact verified-leader registry rule as law and re-scopes YZXCONV-036 to catalog-before-run (targets pinned and frozen before any task executes)
decisions.supersedes_flag: DEC-YZXCONV-008 part B (was flagged 'for owner confirmation') is hereby CONFIRMED and is no longer provisional.
decisions.decision: Owner CONFIRMS DEC-YZXCONV-008 part B: the per-artifact VERIFIED LEADING REGISTRY rule is ratified as LAW and is no longer flagged/provisional. Owner intent restated verbatim: 'you got my intent - the source with the latest code wins. The code is the source of truth.' Two operative laws follow. (A) VERIFIED-LEADER-PER-ARTIFACT LAW: the catalog records, for every artifact, the registry that is empirically LEADING (newest real code) resolved live at pin time - crates.io for the RuVector engine (ruvector-core, ruvllm, ruvector-postgres), npm for the TS/Bun front-end binding (ruvector / @ruvector/*) only, and normal crates.io for independent upstreams (redb, candle, sqlx, libsql) - never a blanket npm-always-wins and never a blanket crates-always-wins. DEC-YZXCONV-007 #3's 'ruvector = npm latest' remains true but SCOPED to the front-end binding artifact; the engine's leader is verified per snapshot (crates.io currently leads). (B) CATALOG-BEFORE-RUN LAW (the owner's 'more reason to set the supply chain target in the catalog before the tasks are run'): YZXCONV-036 is RE-SCOPED from 'seed the inventory' into a PRECONDITION GATE. The supply_chain.catalog.csv target for every artifact - source_registry + source_locator + pinned_version, each verified live - MUST be set and FROZEN before any downstream supply-chain task (YZXCONV-035 package postgres+ruvector, YZXCONV-037 ruvllm lane, YZXCONV-038 embedding asset) executes. Downstream tasks CONSUME the frozen catalog target and never re-derive it. A task that installs, packages, or links an artifact whose catalog target is unset or unverified is a NO-OPTIONAL / black-hole violation and fails closed. Consequence in the task graph: YZXCONV-035 now depends on YZXCONV-036 (previously 033;034 -> now 033;034;036) so the freeze precedes packaging; 037 and 038 already depend on 036. The catalog is the single source of the pinned target; the code (latest, verified) is the source of truth the catalog points at.
evidence: Owner message 2026-07-14: 'confirm the per-artifact verified-leader rule; re-scope YZXCONV-036; you got my intent - the source with the latest code wins. The code is the source of truth; this is more reason to set the supply chain target in the catalog before the tasks are run.'

DEC-YZXCONV-008 part B (the flagged empirical correction now confirmed): crates.io leads the RuVector engine (ruvector-core 2.3.0, ruvllm 2.3.0, ruvector-postgres 2.0.5 pgrx); npm carries only the binding (ruvector 0.2.34, @ruvector/* 2.x); @ruvnet/ruvector is a dead 404 handle
Task-graph re-scope applied in task_graph.source.csv: YZXCONV-036 title/goal/verification_gate now a freeze-before-run target gate; YZXCONV-035 parent_id 'YZXCONV-033; YZXCONV-034; YZXCONV-036'
Architecture Blueprint lines 87-113 (latest code wins; CI publishes on merge) and DEC-YZXCONV-007 north-star hash sha256:014bbebb...

decisions.title: Blueprint deep re-scan: full component coverage map, git source-axis for FlexNetOS engine crates, TEAS integration evidence, realized-tier forensics, and the surfaced GitHub App
decisions.decided_by: Agent (deep re-scan loop, owner-directed) - four parallel agents: blueprint re-read, heavy-folder forensics, gitkb code-intel + task scan, github-app surface
decisions.decision: Owner-directed 'keep scanning the blueprint for everything you missed' + scan the 3 integrated worktrees + explain the heavy folders + run gitkb code-intel + surface the envctl GitHub App. Five findings, all landed in SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md sections 8e/10/11/12. (A) FULL COMPONENT COVERAGE (section 10 gap map): a section-by-section re-read of all 707 lines surfaced the complete component set beyond the prior pass - ruvllm-on-Candle, AgentDB/.rvf, RuVector-as-PG-extension (ruvector-core/sona), RuvLTRA proportional router (trained classifier head), Ruflo meta-harness, ATAS (Echo-State-Network/Reservoir-Computing, 100-step ensemble), RuVector MinCut (subpolynomial, arXiv:2512.13105), SHAKE256 witness chains, Causal Graph Verify (GNN+Cypher), COW branching, SONA/FastGRNN, redb MVCC/ACID/BLAKE3-dedup, nu_plugin_ruvector (MessagePack), envctl projection (rows to .rs/.toml/.nu via module_path), LifeOS+Bun/bunx FFI over UDS/shared-redb (no HTTP), Nix+musl x86_64-unknown-linux-musl, MicroLoRA rank-1/2 on frozen Llama/Qwen, Thompson/Q-Learning/PPO, 230+ SQL fns, 50+ agents/one footprint. NO component is dropped: each maps to a catalog row (section 8/8e) or an existing task. (B) GIT SOURCE-AXIS (section 8e): the blueprint's engine layer is being built inside FlexNetOS-org repos NOW, so for those artifacts the verified leading registry is the git branch HEAD (source_registry=git, source_locator=github/FlexNetOS/<repo>@<branch>, pin=resolved commit) - the verified-leader law (DEC-009) covers this; these rows are pin-at-036. (C) TEAS INTEGRATION EVIDENCE (section 11): the owner's '3 folders at meta/worktrees' are a coordinated cross-repo build of TEAS wired by seams S1-S6 over handoff.task.v1 - ph--teas-frontdoor (prompt_hub, S1 intent front door), mrv--teas-engine (meta-ruvector rvagent-engine + witnessed proof ledger + the blueprint-named crates, S2/S3/S6), teri--compute-world (execution-effect twin / ATAS analog). The CONTROL SPINE (intent->WorkOrder->witnessed execution) is REALIZED (e2e_teri_compute_world.rs dogfood); the DATA tier (Postgres+RuVector+redb) and RUNTIME tier (LifeOS/Bun/Yazelix/musl) are the two largest GAPS - exactly YZXCONV-035/038/021 + Wave-4. (D) REALIZED-TIER FORENSICS (section 12): meta/var=15G live data tier (two Postgres clusters, ~275M redb code-intel DBs, SQLite agentdb) validating 'data dir OUTSIDE the store'; meta/usr=190M meta FHS binaries with real toolchains delegated to the profile-owned nix store via tiny shims (confirms no-system-install already holds for binaries); /home/flexnetos/workspace=the pre-refactor design source (envctl-is-env-truth doctrine); _quarantine/20260630T234500Z/README.md=refactor-integrity evidence (a prior faked-completion pass was quarantined; recovery requires REAL state) - which is WHY this catalog pins verified, not asserted, registries. (E) GITHUB APP SURFACED (org-access substrate for the auto-merge swarm; not bluffed): envctl ships a real, wired, e2e-tested GitHub App credential broker - App name flexnetos-github-app, App ID 4044997 (public, in docs/runbook + secretctl help + tests), installation 140063898 (policy-drift default), org FlexNetOS. Brokered by secretd (provider-github default feature) via secretctl mint-github / RPC Vault.MintGithub(installation_id,repository_ids,permissions,ttl_secs)->{token,expires_at_unix}; RS256 App-JWT -> installation token. Private key is vault-sealed under secret name github-app-private-key (no PEM in the tree; USB+unlock gated, fail-closed). Evidenced scopes: administration:write + metadata:read (ORG RULES / rulesets / branch protection - the policy-drift token), checks:write + contents:read (checks + auto-merge gate). actions:write/workflows:write are NOT hard-coded - they are passed per-mint as --permissions. OWNER CLARIFICATION 2026-07-14 ('the app is full access for a reason ... the relay broker sends a different key and permission level depending on the target need. That is the design.'): the App holds FULL ACCESS by design and the mint-github --permissions mechanism IS the relay broker scoping DOWN to a different key + permission level per target need, so actions:write/workflows:write ARE mintable (not a gap) - this is the intended least-privilege-at-mint-time design, not a missing scope. Target architecture: the COGNITUM vault hosts a model that gates the secrets with the relay engine; long-lived secrets and tokens exist but are never seen by any human or agent - the relay broker issues a narrowly-scoped short-lived token per request. Caveat retained: the code path is fully wired; whether 4044997/140063898 is currently enrolled in an unlocked vault is runtime state not provable from the tree (the vault/USB-unlock gate is itself part of the design).
evidence: Blueprint re-read agent: 34 components beyond the prior pass with line citations (ruvllm 13/276/453, RuvLTRA 81-85/657-659, Ruflo 127-129/190-200, ATAS/ESN 163-188/465-498, MinCut 206-215 arXiv:2512.13105, SHAKE256 63-66/616, GNN+Cypher 202-204/617, MicroLoRA 281-288, redb 256-262/365-398, nu_plugin MessagePack 145/549, envctl projection 56-61/625-630, Bun FFI/UDS 91-125/356-358, musl 30-42/632-634, 230+ SQL fns 243, 50+ agents 137/276)

3 worktrees: ph--teas-frontdoor=prompt_hub@feat/teas-frontdoor-emitter (teas_frontdoor.rs, IntentLock BLAKE3, commit f26986d4, 77/77); mrv--teas-engine=meta-ruvector@feat/teas-rvagent-engine (rvagent-engine ledger.rs/proof.rs, rvagent-mcp/teas.rs, ruvector-mincut, commits dcfa94855/446cae29e/dab9730a7/7448d506e, e2e_teri_compute_world.rs); teri--compute-world=teri@feat/teri-compute-world (sim/compute_world.rs, compute_predictor.rs graduation to ruv-fann/conformal-prediction/sona/ruvllm, commits 196d31c-6e5891b)

Heavy-folder forensics: meta/var 15G (var/lib/postgresql/17 2.0G + var/lib/ruvector/pgdata 47M PG clusters; codedb-online-node-*.redb ~236M + kb.redb 39M; agentdb/reasoningbank.db); meta/usr 190M FHS (usr/bin meta tools, usr/libexec/envctl 41M; nix/wild/llvm-* 67-194B shims to profile store); workspace=flexnetos_production_execution_pack (envctl-is-env-truth); _quarantine README=faked-completion quarantine, real-state-required recovery

GitKB code-intel: git-kb code 0.2.12 indexed planning-spine-v0 = 1283 symbols / 1471 files / 121 flows / 132 entrypoints; hotspots _common.py root() 262 callers / package_root() 228 / write_json() 91; navigation pipeline build-navigation-index.mjs (47 symbols) + normalize-task-graph.py. GitNexus NOT usable (no .gitnexus/run.cjs on box) - not faked. NOTE the agent read the STALE local main checkout (22 tasks); origin/main + this worktree carry all 38

GitHub App (envctl main checkout /home/flexnetos/meta/src/envctl, read-only, NO secret values read): user_agent flexnetos-github-app (mint_github.rs:208); App ID 4044997 (docs/runbook/README.md:85, secretctl/src/cli.rs:128); installation 140063898 (GITHUB-TRANSPORT-DOCTRINE.md:65); GITHUB_APP_KEY_NAME=github-app-private-key + GITHUB_APP_ID_META=github-app-id (lib.rs:162-164); provider-github default feature (secretd/Cargo.toml:17); grpc mint_github (grpc.rs:393-460) -> engine.mint_github_token (lib.rs:2294-2357); e2e native_mint_e2e.rs (unlock->enroll 4044997->MintGithub->mock 201->GITHUB_TOKEN); scopes administration:write+metadata:read (policy-drift) and checks:write+contents:read (GITHUB-TRANSPORT-DOCTRINE.md:62-87); no actions/workflows scope hard-coded

decisions.title: The single nix-profile is now HARD-GATED (failing flake + live-box checks); Mars is proven inside the one closure (YZXCONV-039/040)
decisions.decided_by: Owner (directive) + Agent (two-agent deep review: yazelix code review, task-graph cross-analysis)
decisions.decision: Owner: 'make sure there are yazelix build tasks that HARD GATE the single nix-profile. I have repeated this several times but the rule is never followed. We are using Mars for now because it is built in and the easy path. Run deep code review on yazelix and cross-analyze the task to ensure it is well covered.' A two-agent deep review (read-only) found WHY the rule was never followed and closed it with two new build tasks. FINDING (yazelix code review of /home/flexnetos/meta/src/yazelix): the intended single profile is lifeos_foundation_yzx (a pkgs.symlinkJoin owning yzx + Mars + every flexnetosExecutables toolchain binary; flake.nix:932-953, 808-901), and Mars is SAFELY baked into that one closure - withMars is a store-path bake-in (marsPath string injected at flake.nix:528-529), NOT a second package set, and both yazelix and lifeos_foundation_yzx are withMars=true so Mars is inside the one symlinkJoin. So the owner's Mars 'easy path' is architecturally single-profile-safe. BUT the invariant is NOT enforced: (gap 1) the one derivation gate that asserts single-owner shape (flexnetos_foundation_contracts: exactly one .desktop, no shadow launcher yzx-desktop-launch/yzx-agent-workspace-launch, all runtime paths under ~/.nix-profile; flake.nix:1236-1364) is NEVER built by CI - ci.yml:45-53 and version_gate.yml:50-54 omit it and nix flake check --no-build only evaluates, never realizes it; (gap 2) EVERY check validates the DERIVATION, never the LIVE BOX - nothing runs nix profile list to assert exactly one profile, nothing scans PATH/~/.local/~/.cargo//usr/npm-global for shadow installs, nothing verifies the resolved binary comes from ~/.nix-profile (yzx doctor only checks its own baked store paths). The flake also publishes FOUR installable roots (yazelix, runtime, default, lifeos_foundation_yzx; flake.nix:954-960) and the generic yazelix root ships a SECOND share/applications/yzx.desktop launcher (flake.nix:1070-1071) - the exact clause-14 launcher-shadow leak - while README/AGENTS instruct installing the generic root. CROSS-ANALYSIS (task graph): the single-profile rule is CONVERGED (YZXCONV-003/006/014/022) and audited by a human (YZXCONV-020, simulation_required=FALSE) but has NO red-first failing gate the way Kache (YZXCONV-016) and Nushell (YZXCONV-017) do; the only red-first profile test (022) is scoped to Home Manager + the desktop launcher, not the whole closure; and Mars had ZERO task coverage (grep of the package returns no withMars/Mars). RESOLUTION - two new REQUIRED build tasks (NO-OPTIONAL), using the graph's proven red-first idiom, wired into the YZXCONV-020 closure (020 parent_id extended with 039;040): YZXCONV-039 hard-gates the single nix-profile - (A) wire flexnetos_foundation_contracts + contracts into the ci.yml build list so they FAIL a PR, (B) add a derivation flake check single_profile_closure (closureInfo over the foundation) asserting one profile owns the union of yzx + every flexnetosExecutables key with exactly one yzx and no non-foundation store path, (C) add a LIVE-BOX gate nushell/system/profile_guard.nu (wired like host_policy.nu into the yazelix_host_policy service) that FAILS on a second profile, an off-profile binary resolution, a non-profile-first PATH, or a second terminal .desktop, (D) reuse the fakeYazelix/fakeYazelixDesktop negative fixtures so the gate self-regresses; YZXCONV-040 proves Mars is baked INTO the one closure (marsPackage present in the foundation closure - the positive dual of the runtime_contracts negative grep at flake.nix:1206 - exactly one mars under ~/.nix-profile, the only terminal .desktop is com.flexnetos.Yazelix.Agent.desktop with Exec ~/.nix-profile/bin/yzx launch, and the Mars-spawned shell is profile-nu), closing the generic-root second-launcher leak. Net: the single-profile+Mars-in-one-closure law moves from a latent, un-run derivation contract to a failing PR gate PLUS the only live-box check that catches an actual second profile / shadow / user-global install - which is what the owner reports is never caught.
evidence: yazelix code review 2026-07-14 (/home/flexnetos/meta/src/yazelix, read-only): flake.nix:932-960 four installable roots + foundation symlinkJoin; 808-901 flexnetosExecutables/flexnetosTools linkFarm; 518-588 mkYzxCommand withMars, 528-529 marsPath bake, 660/921 withMars=true; 1203-1231 runtime_contracts (negative mars grep at 1206); 1236-1364 flexnetos_foundation_contracts (1265-1278 no-shadow/single-desktop/profile-path); 1070-1071 generic yazelix yzx.desktop; 977-995 fakeYazelix/fakeYazelixDesktop fixtures; ci.yml:45-53 + version_gate.yml:50-54 omit foundation_contracts; ci.yml:43 nix flake check --no-build; runtime/yzx/doctor.rs + nushell/system/host_policy.nu (no live-box profile check)

task-graph cross-analysis 2026-07-14: YZXCONV-003/014 assert one profile only as positive runtime/doctor observations; 006/021 scoped single-binary; 020 strongest single-owner wording but simulation_required=FALSE (human audit); 022 is the only red-first profile gate but bounded to Home Manager + desktop launcher; YZXCONV-016/017 prove the red-first failing-gate idiom exists but was never applied to the profile globally; Mars/withMars absent from PLAN.md/README/task_graph

PLAN.md clause 1 (one profile) and clause 14 (no second profile/edit surface/launcher shadow); owner directive 2026-07-14 'hard gate the single nix-profile ... the rule is never followed ... using Mars for now because it is built in and the easy path'

Task-graph wiring: YZXCONV-039 (parents 003;014;022) + YZXCONV-040 (parents 003;022;039) appended; YZXCONV-020 parent_id extended with 039;040 so the final-closure proof depends on both gates; owner-contract test bumped 38->40
